part_1: ^2 AM. Friday dragging itself into Saturday.
^Jack Daniel's in a crystal glass. Neat, no ice. The caramel first, then the maple char — that burnt wood taste that cleans your throat and your head. Especially your head.
^The dispatch center is almost empty tonight. Few operators, all lazy. Calls you can count on one hand.
^"Ma'am, a cat in a tree is not an emergency. We'll notify the fire department."
^A few robberies at the usual corner stores. Booze, food, nothing special. The patrols live off this stuff.
^The headset was bothering me. The screen light burned my eyes.
part_2: ^The LED blinks. One, two, three times.

c-0: ^I wipe my lips. Another sip wouldn't hurt.
^I sigh. No one else will answer for me. Not tonight.

answer: ^I pick up the receiver. The weight of the headset on my skull.
^911, what's the address of your emergency?

part_3: ^Hi... I just wanted to know if the Bear is still angry.
^Sweetie, you've got the wrong number. This line isn't for playing.
^But the Bear is right outside the door. He told me if I talk he'll take my toys away. Are we playing the quiet game?
^The ice in the glass. A sip. Annoyance.
^Listen, hand the phone to your mom or dad. Now.
^They can't. They're sleeping on the carpet.
^There's strawberry juice everywhere... the Bear spilled it all over.
part_4: ^My hands are shaking. The glass is almost empty. I need to say something.

crooked_response: ^Look... I don't have time for stories. If there's no adult, I have to end the call. We have procedures to follow.
^The words come out crooked. The alcohol speaks for me.

part_5: ^But the Bear has my name on his list... I don't want to play anymore.
^Something breaks inside me. Not in the glass. Inside.
^Shit... listen to me. This isn't a game. You need to hide. Now. Get under the bed. Don't breathe loud.
^He's right outside the door... he's scratching the wood.
^I can see his shoes. They're black and shiny, like daddy's when we go to church.
^The realization hits me like a punch.
^It's not a game. It was never a game.
^The parents on the carpet. The strawberry juice. The Bear.
^Close your eyes. Don't look at the shoes. Think of... think of a nice place.
^I need to know who she is. I need to—
part_6_name: ^The door opens. I hear it through the line.
part_6_anonymous: ^I open my mouth. Nothing comes out.
^The door opens. I hear it through the line.
